description: Static golden for the cross-binding dependency-partition (cadence) contract (CONFORMANCE_SPEC.md §5.7, RFC semiring-faq-unified-ir §6.1). Each fixture pins the golden the partition pass MUST reproduce in every binding: the cadence CLASS of every annotated node (const ⊏ discrete ⊏ continuous, class(node)=max over inputs, the gather rule classing index expressions independently of the array), the SET of materialization points (where the frontier cut fires — a lower-cadence sub-DAG feeding a higher-cadence parent), and the byte-identical CONST-folded buffers. The three fixtures are the §6.1 trio: a mixed stencil (all three classes, both frontier thresholds, the gather split), a pure-topology rule (all CONST, empty hot tree — folds entirely into the artifact), and a pure-pointwise rule (all CONTINUOUS, empty per-event handler, no materialization). Until the per-binding partition-pass implementations land (ess-my4.3.7 Julia, and the Rust/Python siblings), the harness (scripts/run-cadence-conformance.py --self-test) asserts the contract against an embedded REFERENCE classifier + folder; each binding then registers a thin adapter and the SAME golden is asserted across Julia / Rust / Python. See tests/conformance/cadence/README.md for the adapter contract.

fixtures.doc: The canonical three-cadence FVM stencil D(u[i])/dt = Σ_k Kdiff[i]·coeff[i,k]·(u[nbr[i,k]] − u[i]). CONST topology (nbr, coeff) folds into the artifact; the DISCRETE diffusivity Kdiff materializes into a per-event buffer; the CONTINUOUS state loads form the hot tree. The nested gather index(u, index(nbr,i,k)) splits: the inner topology selection is CONST while the outer value load is CONTINUOUS.

materialization_points.label: nbr_idx
materialization_points.doc: The CONST topology gather index(nbr,i,k) feeding the CONTINUOUS value load index(u, .) — folds at compile into a 0-based neighbour-index buffer the hot path reads as a constant.

materialization_points.label: coeff
materialization_points.doc: The CONST coefficient gather index(coeff,i,k) feeding the CONTINUOUS product — folds at compile into a coefficient buffer.

materialization_points.label: Kdiff
materialization_points.doc: The DISCRETE diffusivity gather index(Kdiff,i) feeding the CONTINUOUS product — materialized into a per-event buffer, recomputed by the per-event handler when the source refreshes (schedule). No compile-time golden bytes (the value comes from the runtime data source); asserted by the per-binding adapter at the DISCRETE refresh epoch.

fixtures.doc: The minimal 2-triangle mesh (faces sharing edge {2,3}). The unique undirected edges are enumerated from the CONST face→vertex connectivity and ranked to dense ids. Every node is CONST, so the entire partition folds into the artifact at compile time: the per-step CONTINUOUS tree is EMPTY and the per-event DISCRETE handler is EMPTY. Reuses the determinism edge_enumeration golden (tests/conformance/determinism/manifest.json) — the dedup order and numbering are fixed by the §5.5 determinism rules.

materialization_points.label: edge_set
materialization_points.doc: The CONST distinct/skolem aggregate that materializes the derived `edges` index set — folds into the artifact at compile time (the deduplicated, canonically-sorted edge set).

materialization_points.label: edge_dense_id
materialization_points.doc: The CONST rank that assigns dense ids over `edges` — folds into the artifact (the array-backend index buffer).

fixtures.doc: A pointwise relaxation-plus-forcing RHS D(u[i])/dt = −u[i] + A·sin(omega·t). Every node is CONTINUOUS — including the analytic continuous-t forcing sin(omega·t), which is NOT piecewise-constant between events and must recompute every step (classing it DISCRETE would let it go stale). The CONST scalars A, omega inline as literals (the pre-existing constant-fold); a bare scalar constant is not a buffer, so the materialization-point SET is empty, the per-event handler is empty, and the entire RHS is the hot tree.
